did_this_paper_use_ai_to_obtain_results_.details: The paper uses an improved Arithmetic Optimization Algorithm (ETAOA), a meta-heuristic optimization algorithm, to perform multi-threshold image segmentation on skin cancer dermoscopic images. This computational method is applied to segment lesions in dermatology images to aid in computer-aided diagnosis.

is_this_paper_a_review_paper_or_a_resear.details: The document is explicitly labeled as a 'Research Article' in the header. It presents original work proposing an improved arithmetic optimization algorithm (ETAOA) for multi-threshold image segmentation. The abstract and introduction describe specific experimental methodologies, including mechanism comparison experiments, scalability tests, and segmentation experiments on skin cancer dermoscopic images, which are characteristic of original research rather than a review.

title: A multi-threshold image segmentation method based on arithmetic optimization algorithm: A real case with skin cancer dermoscopic images
journal: Journal of Computational Design and Engineering
brief_summary: This study proposes an improved Arithmetic Optimization Algorithm (ETAOA) enhanced with elite evolutionary and tracking strategies to solve the multi-threshold image segmentation problem in skin cancer dermoscopic images. The algorithm was validated using CEC2014 and CEC2022 benchmark functions, demonstrating superior optimization performance compared to nine state-of-the-art peers. For dermatological application, the method was tested on skin cancer dermoscopy images provided by The First Affiliated Hospital of Ningbo University. Evaluation under both low (6, 8, 10) and high (15, 20, 25) threshold conditions utilized metrics such as Peak Signal-to-Noise Ratio and Structural Similarity Index. The results indicate that ETAOA achieves superior segmentation performance and ranks first among compared methods, effectively handling complex threshold selection for medical image analysis.

lesion_types_studied.details: The paper focuses on the segmentation of skin cancer lesions in dermoscopic images provided by The First Affiliated Hospital of Ningbo University. Specific histological subtypes (e.g., melanoma, BCC) are not explicitly named; the study treats 'skin cancer' as the general clinical entity for segmentation evaluation.

all_task_types.target: Skin cancer lesions in dermoscopic images
all_task_types.details: The paper focuses on multi-threshold image segmentation (MTIS) of skin cancer dermoscopic images. The method uses an improved Arithmetic Optimization Algorithm (ETAOA) to optimize threshold selection for dividing images into disjoint regions (segmentation masks). Evaluation metrics include Peak Signal-to-Noise Ratio (PSNR), Structural Similarity Index (SSIM), and Feature Similarity Index (FSIM), which measure the quality of the segmented images against the original images.

datasets_used.name: CEC2014 and CEC2022 Benchmark Functions
datasets_used.description: Standard benchmark functions used to verify the optimization performance of the proposed ETAOA algorithm through mechanism comparison experiments, scalability tests, and comparison with nine state-of-the-art peers.

use.training: Used for mechanism comparison experiments and scalability tests to verify optimization performance.
use.validation: Used for comparison experiments with nine state-of-the-art peers.
use.testing: Used for mechanism comparison experiments and scalability tests.

datasets_used.name: Skin Cancer Dermoscopy Images
datasets_used.description: Real-world dermoscopy images of skin cancer provided by The First Affiliated Hospital of Ningbo University, Ningbo, Zhejiang, China. The dataset contains noisy real hospital image data used for segmentation experiments.

use.training: Used for segmentation experiments to demonstrate the feasibility of ETAOA in the segmentation domain.
use.validation: Used for segmentation experiments under low (6, 8, 10) and high (15, 20, 25) threshold conditions.
use.testing: Used for segmentation experiments under low (6, 8, 10) and high (15, 20, 25) threshold conditions.
use.other: Real-world application case for computer-aided diagnosis.

statistical_methods_used.purpose: To compare the performance of the proposed ETAOA algorithm against the original AOA and nine other state-of-the-art algorithms on CEC2022 benchmark functions and skin cancer dermoscopy images, providing statistical significance of the differences in results.

To rank the performance of the proposed ETAOA algorithm against the original AOA and nine other state-of-the-art algorithms across multiple benchmark functions and image datasets to determine overall superiority.

hypothesis_testing.null_hypothesis: There is no significant difference in performance between the proposed ETAOA algorithm and the compared algorithms (AOA or nine state-of-the-art peers).
hypothesis_testing.alternative_hypothesis: There is a significant difference in performance between the proposed ETAOA algorithm and the compared algorithms.
hypothesis_testing.interpretation: The WSRT was used to provide statistical significance of the differences in results between ETAOA and other algorithms on CEC2022 benchmark functions and skin cancer dermoscopy images. The results indicated that ETAOA ranked first in composite scores, showing stronger robustness and superior segmentation performance compared to the other methods.

CEC2022_Benchmark_Functions.notes: ETAOA ranked first in composite scores on CEC2022 benchmark functions, demonstrating superior optimization performance compared to nine state-of-the-art peers. The paper also reports WSRT results for ETAOA vs AOA on CEC2022 across dimensions 10, 50, 150, and 200, where ETAOA consistently ranked 1st with a mean rank of 1.083 compared to AOA's 1.750.

Skin_Cancer_Dermoscopy_Images.dataset_source: The First Affiliated Hospital of Ningbo University
Skin_Cancer_Dermoscopy_Images.experiment_setting: Multi-threshold image segmentation (MTIS) under high thresholds (15, 20, 25) and low thresholds (6, 8, 10). Each experiment iterated 100 times, conducted independently 30 times. Image size: 512x512. Population size: 20. Objective function: 2D Rényi’s entropy using NL-means 2D histogram.

ETAOA.high_threshold_ranking: Ranked first in composite score across high threshold levels (15, 20, 25)
ETAOA.low_threshold_ranking: Ranked first in all segmentation evaluation results across low threshold levels (6, 8, 10)
ETAOA.notes: ETAOA showed superior segmentation performance, stronger robustness, and better detail preservation compared to AOA, DE, HHO, PSO, RIME, BLPSO, CCACO, CLPSO, IGWO, and LGCMFO.

AOA.notes: Compared against ETAOA; ETAOA showed accelerated convergence and superior optimal solutions.

Skin_Cancer_Dermoscopy_Images.notes: Specific numerical metric values (PSNR, SSIM, FSIM) for individual images are not provided in the text context; they are referenced as being in Tables A3-A8 (high thresholds) and Tables A6-A8 (low thresholds). The paper states ETAOA achieved the highest quality segmentation results and ranked first in composite scores for both high and low threshold conditions.

architecture_used.main_model: ETAOA (Improved Arithmetic Optimization Algorithm with Elite Evolutionary Strategy and Elite Tracking Strategy)

machine_learning_or_deep_learning_models: ETAOA (Improved Arithmetic Optimization Algorithm with Elite Evolutionary Strategy and Elite Tracking Strategy)
CCACO (Chimp Colony Artificial Bee Colony Optimization)
LGCMFO (Lévy Flight Grey Wolf Moth-Flame Optimization)
preprocessing_techniques: NL-means 2D histogram representation for image information
2D Rényi’s entropy as objective function for threshold selection
conclusions: The proposed Improved Arithmetic Optimization Algorithm (ETAOA), enhanced with Elite Evolutionary Strategy (EES) and Elite Tracking Strategy (ETS), demonstrates superior optimization performance compared to the original AOA and nine other state-of-the-art meta-heuristic algorithms on CEC2014 and CEC2022 benchmark functions.

ETAOA achieves the highest ranking in composite scores and segmentation evaluation metrics (PSNR, SSIM, FSIM) for multi-threshold image segmentation of skin cancer dermoscopic images under both low (6, 8, 10) and high (15, 20, 25) threshold conditions.

The introduction of EES and ETS positively improves the optimization performance of the base AOA, leading to accelerated convergence and stronger robustness against premature convergence issues observed in the original AOA.

ETAOA effectively handles the exponential growth in time complexity associated with multi-threshold image segmentation by optimizing threshold selection using 2D Rényi’s entropy based on NL-means 2D histograms.

The method shows strong generalizability across different threshold levels and benchmark functions, maintaining superior performance in both mechanism comparison experiments and real-world dermatological image segmentation tasks.
important_remarks: The study relies on a small, single-center dataset of only 10 skin cancer dermoscopy images from The First Affiliated Hospital of Ningbo University, limiting generalizability and raising concerns about dataset bias.

No external validation was performed; the method was only tested on the internal hospital dataset and standard optimization benchmarks (CEC2014/2022), not on widely used public dermatology datasets like ISIC.

Specific quantitative performance metrics (PSNR, SSIM, FSIM values) for the dermatological segmentation are not reported in the main text, preventing independent verification of the claimed superiority.

The paper acknowledges that the proposed ETAOA algorithm has increased computational complexity compared to the base AOA, suggesting a need for distributed or high-performance computing for deployment.

The algorithm is a meta-heuristic optimizer for threshold selection rather than a deep learning model, which may limit its ability to learn complex semantic features compared to modern CNN-based segmentation methods.
The study does not report statistical significance tests (e.g., p-values) for the dermatological segmentation results, only for the benchmark function comparisons.
